findings.title: Content-Security-Policy Header Omitted
findings.observation: No Content-Security-Policy (CSP) header was observed on the response.
findings.aiAnalysis: No CSP header was observed on the scanned response. However, no active XSS exploit vector was successfully demonstrated. The finding is treated as a best-practice hardening recommendation rather than evidence of exploitable risk.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: A missing CSP header is a common observation. Modern client-side frameworks and CDNs may mitigate this. In the absence of demonstrated XSS, this remains a hardening advisory. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
findings.businessImpact: Lack of CSP increases the impact of future script injection (XSS) bugs, allowing session hijacking.
findings.remediation: Implement a strict Content-Security-Policy header, setting default-src to self and whitelisting trusted hosts.

findings.description: No Content-Security-Policy (CSP) header was observed on the response.
findings.reasoning: No CSP header was observed on the scanned response. However, no active XSS exploit vector was successfully demonstrated. The finding is treated as a best-practice hardening recommendation rather than evidence of exploitable risk.

findings.title: Missing X-Frame-Options Header
findings.observation: The X-Frame-Options header is missing, which could theoretically allow the page to be framed inside external websites.
findings.detectionLogic: Verify absence of both x-frame-options and CSP frame-ancestors
findings.aiAnalysis: No clickjacking protections (X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors) were observed. However, no clickjacking scenario or state-changing action was demonstrated.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: Clickjacking requires a high-value state-changing transaction (like a submit button or delete button) that can be abused. In its absence, this is a hardening advisory. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
findings.businessImpact: Exposes users to clickjacking attacks if they interact with the page inside an attacker-controlled iframe.
findings.remediation: Set the X-Frame-Options header to DENY or SAMEORIGIN, or add a frame-ancestors directive to your CSP.

findings.reasoning: No clickjacking protections (X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors) were observed. However, no clickjacking scenario or state-changing action was demonstrated.

findings.title: Missing X-Content-Type-Options Header
findings.observation: The X-Content-Type-Options header is missing, allowing browsers to MIME-sniff response content types.
findings.aiAnalysis: No X-Content-Type-Options header was observed. Browsers may attempt to parse files (like images or user uploads) as JavaScript, presenting an XSS vector if file upload controls are weak.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: Without a file upload vector or injection sink, MIME sniffing does not present a direct exploit path. Downgraded to Best Practice. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
findings.businessImpact: Low. Only creates risk if users can upload arbitrary files that the browser might parse as script.
findings.remediation: Configure your web server to return X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff for all HTTP responses.

findings.title: Missing Referrer-Policy Header
findings.observation: The Referrer-Policy header is missing, which could leak sensitive parameters or tokens in referrer headers to external hosts.
findings.aiAnalysis: No Referrer-Policy header was observed. The browser's default behavior applies, which might send URL paths including query strings to third parties.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: If the site does not contain sensitive tokens or IDs in URL paths or parameters, the risk of data leakage is negligible. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
findings.businessImpact: Potential leakage of sensitive parameters or tokens to external sites through HTTP Referer headers.

findings.title: Missing Permissions-Policy Header
findings.observation: The Permissions-Policy header is missing, leaving browser API feature permissions unrestricted.
findings.aiAnalysis: No Permissions-Policy header was observed. Interactive browser APIs (camera, geolocation, microphone) can be accessed by the site or framed third-party scripts.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: If the application does not load third-party scripts or handle sensitive device sensors, the practical risk is minimal. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
findings.businessImpact: Enables loaded scripts to request access to browser device capabilities without restrictions.
findings.remediation: Implement a Permissions-Policy header restricting access to browser capabilities not utilized by the application.

findings.title: HTTP Method Disabled: PUT
findings.observation: Testing the PUT method returned status 404, indicating it is disabled or unavailable at this path.
findings.detectionLogic: Check if HTTP status is outside 2xx/401/403
findings.aiAnalysis: The server rejected the method request, confirming that anonymous modifications are blocked.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: The server returned an explicit method rejection status. Safe behavior. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]

findings.title: Missing HTTPS Encryption
findings.observation: The target application is hosted over HTTP. Traffic is transmitted in plain text, making it vulnerable to sniffing.
findings.detectionLogic: Check if URL scheme matches http:// and redirection is absent
findings.aiAnalysis: The target site accepts plain text connections and does not automatically redirect to HTTPS, leaving user traffic completely unencrypted and vulnerable to MITM attacks.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: Verified that requests do not upgrade. This is a confirmed vulnerability for production applications.
findings.businessImpact: Severe risk of credential sniffing, session hijacking, and traffic tampering by network attackers.
findings.remediation: Configure the web server to enforce SSL/TLS and redirect all HTTP connections to HTTPS.
findings.description: The target application is hosted over HTTP. Traffic is transmitted in plain text, making it vulnerable to sniffing.
findings.reasoning: The target site accepts plain text connections and does not automatically redirect to HTTPS, leaving user traffic completely unencrypted and vulnerable to MITM attacks.

findings.title: Sensitive Directories Protected
findings.observation: None of the tested sensitive configuration paths returned exposed data or false positive response pages.
findings.detectionLogic: No paths returned 200/403 with valid content signatures
findings.aiAnalysis: All directory checks completed without exposing critical config files.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: All probed paths successfully returned standard 404 or blocked responses. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
findings.businessImpact: None. Server paths are protected from scanning attacks.
findings.description: None of the tested sensitive configuration paths returned exposed data or false positive response pages.
findings.reasoning: All directory checks completed without exposing critical config files.

findings.title: Reflected XSS Checks Passed
findings.observation: No unescaped reflection of scripting payloads was detected in the tested input fields.
findings.evidence: Probed parameters q, search, name, input with standard XSS script alert.
findings.detectionLogic: Ensure parameter reflections are either absent or properly escaped
findings.aiAnalysis: Tested input parameters did not reflect raw scripting tags.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: Input reflections are properly encoded, or variables are ignored by the application backend. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
findings.remediation: Ensure strict contextual output escaping is maintained.
findings.description: No unescaped reflection of scripting payloads was detected in the tested input fields.
findings.reasoning: Tested input parameters did not reflect raw scripting tags.

findings.title: SQL Injection Checks Passed
findings.observation: No database query errors or SQL syntax leaks were detected in response to dynamic probes.
findings.evidence: Probed parameters id, user, username, query, q, search, product with 4 SQL injection sequences.
findings.detectionLogic: No database errors or WAF blocks triggered
findings.aiAnalysis: The tested application did not leak any database exceptions or errors.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: Parameters successfully parsed without triggering database parser exceptions. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
findings.remediation: Continue enforcing SQL parameterization across all query operations.
findings.description: No database query errors or SQL syntax leaks were detected in response to dynamic probes.
findings.reasoning: The tested application did not leak any database exceptions or errors.

findings.title: Open Redirect Checks Passed
findings.observation: No arbitrary redirection to external evil domains was detected during parameters probing.
findings.evidence: Tested 8 typical redirect parameters with external domain payload.
findings.detectionLogic: Confirm Location header is either absent or stays within the origin domain
findings.aiAnalysis: Redirect requests were either ignored or did not point to the external test domain.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: No parameter redirection bypasses matched the target host validation rules. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
findings.description: No arbitrary redirection to external evil domains was detected during parameters probing.
findings.reasoning: Redirect requests were either ignored or did not point to the external test domain.

findings.title: Loose CORS Policy (Wildcard ACAO)
findings.observation: The Access-Control-Allow-Origin header is set to a wildcard (*), allowing any site to perform cross-origin reads.
findings.aiAnalysis: For public endpoints (web fonts, CDN assets), a wildcard is acceptable. For internal assets, this is loose.
findings.falsePositiveAssessment: If this endpoint serves public assets, a wildcard CORS is correct and expected. It is reported as Best Practice. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
findings.businessImpact: Cross-origin reads are permitted. Low risk if no private data is served.
findings.remediation: Specify specific domain names in Access-Control-Allow-Origin instead of using a wildcard (*).

allFindings.aiAnalysis: No clickjacking protections (X-Frame-Options or CSP frame-ancestors) were observed. However, no clickjacking scenario or state-changing action was demonstrated.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: Clickjacking requires a high-value state-changing transaction (like a submit button or delete button) that can be abused. In its absence, this is a hardening advisory. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
allFindings.businessImpact: Exposes users to clickjacking attacks if they interact with the page inside an attacker-controlled iframe.
allFindings.remediation: Set the X-Frame-Options header to DENY or SAMEORIGIN, or add a frame-ancestors directive to your CSP.

allFindings.title: Missing X-Content-Type-Options Header
allFindings.observation: The X-Content-Type-Options header is missing, allowing browsers to MIME-sniff response content types.
allFindings.aiAnalysis: No X-Content-Type-Options header was observed. Browsers may attempt to parse files (like images or user uploads) as JavaScript, presenting an XSS vector if file upload controls are weak.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: Without a file upload vector or injection sink, MIME sniffing does not present a direct exploit path. Downgraded to Best Practice. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
allFindings.businessImpact: Low. Only creates risk if users can upload arbitrary files that the browser might parse as script.
allFindings.remediation: Configure your web server to return X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff for all HTTP responses.

allFindings.businessImpact: Potential leakage of sensitive parameters or tokens to external sites through HTTP Referer headers.

allFindings.title: Missing Permissions-Policy Header
allFindings.observation: The Permissions-Policy header is missing, leaving browser API feature permissions unrestricted.
allFindings.aiAnalysis: No Permissions-Policy header was observed. Interactive browser APIs (camera, geolocation, microphone) can be accessed by the site or framed third-party scripts.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: If the application does not load third-party scripts or handle sensitive device sensors, the practical risk is minimal. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
allFindings.businessImpact: Enables loaded scripts to request access to browser device capabilities without restrictions.
allFindings.remediation: Implement a Permissions-Policy header restricting access to browser capabilities not utilized by the application.

allFindings.title: Missing HTTPS Encryption
allFindings.observation: The target application is hosted over HTTP. Traffic is transmitted in plain text, making it vulnerable to sniffing.
allFindings.detectionLogic: Check if URL scheme matches http:// and redirection is absent
allFindings.aiAnalysis: The target site accepts plain text connections and does not automatically redirect to HTTPS, leaving user traffic completely unencrypted and vulnerable to MITM attacks.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: Verified that requests do not upgrade. This is a confirmed vulnerability for production applications.
allFindings.businessImpact: Severe risk of credential sniffing, session hijacking, and traffic tampering by network attackers.
allFindings.remediation: Configure the web server to enforce SSL/TLS and redirect all HTTP connections to HTTPS.
allFindings.description: The target application is hosted over HTTP. Traffic is transmitted in plain text, making it vulnerable to sniffing.
allFindings.reasoning: The target site accepts plain text connections and does not automatically redirect to HTTPS, leaving user traffic completely unencrypted and vulnerable to MITM attacks.

allFindings.title: Sensitive Directories Protected
allFindings.observation: None of the tested sensitive configuration paths returned exposed data or false positive response pages.
allFindings.detectionLogic: No paths returned 200/403 with valid content signatures
allFindings.aiAnalysis: All directory checks completed without exposing critical config files.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: All probed paths successfully returned standard 404 or blocked responses. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
allFindings.businessImpact: None. Server paths are protected from scanning attacks.
allFindings.description: None of the tested sensitive configuration paths returned exposed data or false positive response pages.
allFindings.reasoning: All directory checks completed without exposing critical config files.

allFindings.title: Reflected XSS Checks Passed
allFindings.observation: No unescaped reflection of scripting payloads was detected in the tested input fields.
allFindings.evidence: Probed parameters q, search, name, input with standard XSS script alert.
allFindings.detectionLogic: Ensure parameter reflections are either absent or properly escaped
allFindings.aiAnalysis: Tested input parameters did not reflect raw scripting tags.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: Input reflections are properly encoded, or variables are ignored by the application backend. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
allFindings.remediation: Ensure strict contextual output escaping is maintained.
allFindings.description: No unescaped reflection of scripting payloads was detected in the tested input fields.
allFindings.reasoning: Tested input parameters did not reflect raw scripting tags.

allFindings.title: SQL Injection Checks Passed
allFindings.observation: No database query errors or SQL syntax leaks were detected in response to dynamic probes.
allFindings.evidence: Probed parameters id, user, username, query, q, search, product with 4 SQL injection sequences.
allFindings.detectionLogic: No database errors or WAF blocks triggered
allFindings.aiAnalysis: The tested application did not leak any database exceptions or errors.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: Parameters successfully parsed without triggering database parser exceptions. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
allFindings.remediation: Continue enforcing SQL parameterization across all query operations.
allFindings.description: No database query errors or SQL syntax leaks were detected in response to dynamic probes.
allFindings.reasoning: The tested application did not leak any database exceptions or errors.

allFindings.title: Open Redirect Checks Passed
allFindings.observation: No arbitrary redirection to external evil domains was detected during parameters probing.
allFindings.evidence: Tested 8 typical redirect parameters with external domain payload.
allFindings.detectionLogic: Confirm Location header is either absent or stays within the origin domain
allFindings.aiAnalysis: Redirect requests were either ignored or did not point to the external test domain.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: No parameter redirection bypasses matched the target host validation rules. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
allFindings.description: No arbitrary redirection to external evil domains was detected during parameters probing.
allFindings.reasoning: Redirect requests were either ignored or did not point to the external test domain.

allFindings.title: Loose CORS Policy (Wildcard ACAO)
allFindings.observation: The Access-Control-Allow-Origin header is set to a wildcard (*), allowing any site to perform cross-origin reads.
allFindings.aiAnalysis: For public endpoints (web fonts, CDN assets), a wildcard is acceptable. For internal assets, this is loose.
allFindings.falsePositiveAssessment: If this endpoint serves public assets, a wildcard CORS is correct and expected. It is reported as Best Practice. [Validation Review: Downgraded to Informational due to negligible exploit path proof.]
allFindings.businessImpact: Cross-origin reads are permitted. Low risk if no private data is served.
allFindings.remediation: Specify specific domain names in Access-Control-Allow-Origin instead of using a wildcard (*).
